content:
The claim that Avril Lavigne died in 2003 is a well-known internet conspiracy theory, but it is not true. Avril Lavigne, the Canadian singer-songwriter, is alive and has continued her music career well beyond 2003. 

The conspiracy theory suggests that Avril Lavigne died shortly after releasing her debut album "Let Go" in 2002 and was replaced by a look-alike named "Melissa." This theory is based on supposed differences in appearance, style, and personality between early and later photos and videos of Avril. However, there is no credible evidence to support this, and Avril Lavigne herself, as well as her family and representatives, have denied these rumors multiple times.

In reality, Avril Lavigne has released several albums after 2003, performed live, and appeared publicly many times. The theory is an example of an internet myth that gained traction but is unfounded. If you want to know more about her life and career, I can provide accurate information.
